def: A temporary state of mind or feeling.
Not tight or firm; also relaxed and easy-going.
An empty space or missing part where something should be.
On more than one occasion.
A combination of different things mixed together.
One more time.
The outer or top layer of something.
Frequently; many times.
Running side by side at an equal distance; also at the same time.
The head of government in many countries.
Something treated as more important than others.
From one side to the other; diagonally across.
An area of short, cut grass.
To make something go down in level or amount.
During the time that; on condition that.
To the extent that; up to a point.
A feeling of suspense; also electrical voltage.
A place for storing things; also computer memory.
Firm and steady; not likely to change or fall.
A rise in amount, level, or degree.
The way the parts of something are arranged.
Relating to machines, tools, or a particular skill.
How deep something is.
Following long-established customs.
Able or likely to cause death.
The land along the edge of a river, lake, or sea.
Used with 'je' to strengthen a comparison.
Lower than or under something.
Not injured.
Instead; on the contrary.
In front; before others in place or time.
To tell someone about a possible danger.
The way things really are, not as imagined.
By which means; as a result of which.
To an excessive degree; far too.
Popular and in style right now.
The art and study of designing buildings.
An arranged list or formation; also the act of setting something up.
A shaking or trembling of the ground.
A person who goes along with another.
A person who watches something closely.
A statement that proves something is true or agreed.
A name or word used for something.
A hard brown metal made from copper and tin.
Written records; also a factual film about a real subject.
Producing the result you want; also 'actually'.
Advice suggesting that something is good or worth doing.
The act of dismissing someone from a job or letting them go.
A gas found underground and burned as fuel.
A woman with deep knowledge or skill in a field.
The way something is shaped, arranged, or designed.
A piece of land almost surrounded by water.
Against the law.
The chasing and catching of wild animals.
A woman who stands for a job or election.
A large formal meeting; also a country's law-making body.
To point out the faults in someone or something.
Relating to the body rather than the mind.
A metal disc given as a prize or honour.
Having great power or strength.
The hard work or difficulty involved in doing something.
The food that a person or animal needs to live.
The set of qualities that make a person who they are.
One of the tiny dots that make up a digital image.
Happening smoothly, without any difficulty.
A fixed share or number; also a rate or ratio.
A vehicle driven by burning fuel, used in space or as a weapon.
The return match between two teams in a two-game contest.
The state of carrying a developing baby in the body.
A person who looks after sheep.
An event that shocks people and causes public anger.
A long, narrow band of colour or material.
Demanding that rules be obeyed; also harsh.
Something that interrupts or breaks the normal order.
In or toward the south.
Not likely to happen or be true.
To make something smaller in amount or degree.
The way something is shared out or spread.
To make something available to the public.
If nothing more; as a minimum.
For which purpose or reason.
After which; according to which.
An area marked out for a particular purpose.
Happening by chance, not planned.
To fail to notice something.
The sending of signals; also a live broadcast.
As it seems; seemingly.
To keep someone or something safe from harm.
Having drunk too much alcohol.
Over or above that (informal).
Agreeing with a plan or idea.
To get away from a place or danger.
To kill someone by shooting.
Lasting forever; without end.
To make use of something for a purpose.
A large, untidy mass of things piled together.
In religion, the place of punishment after death; also a terrible situation.
A hinged cover over an opening; also slang for the mouth.
An informal word used to hesitate or soften what follows.
Something said or done to make people laugh.
Very bad; causing horror or distress.
An informal sound expressing hesitation or resignation.
Without charge; also for no result.
Not guilty of a crime or wrongdoing.
A smaller title below the main one; also on-screen text of speech.
A feeling of enjoyment or delight.
Crazy; also used informally to mean 'incredibly'.
An agreement to risk money on the outcome of an event.
Amusing; making you laugh.
On or about which; used in questions and clauses.
About or from which.
Extremely beautiful.
The ceremony of burying a dead person.
Protection from attack; also defending in sport.
The absence of light.
Done quickly; in a hurry.
To bear something painful or difficult without giving up.
A person who lacks courage.
A person who fights in battles, especially in the past.
The use of mysterious powers to make impossible things happen.
Unusual and hard to explain.
A small handgun.
A person who leads religious ceremonies.
A word said when raising glasses before drinking.
To do something that might lead to loss or harm.
Stylish and smart in appearance.
A fight between armed forces in a war.
The bony frame of the head.
A stupid or clumsy person (informal).
A person who commits crimes.
